shell.navigationDescription: Primary navigation for AI Development Environment.

runs.activityDescription: Live, searchable provider and tool activity for this run.
runs.attachmentLimits: Up to 25 MB per file and 100 MB total.
runs.attachmentsTooLarge: The selected attachments exceed the 100 MB input limit.
runs.changesSnapshotsDescription: Git checkpoints captured during this run.
runs.crossProviderWarning: Hidden provider state cannot transfer between tools. Choose the visible transcript or final summary as context.
runs.deleteDescription: Native provider history will be deleted before this local record. This cannot be undone.
runs.deleteDraftDescription: Permanently delete the selected draft? This cannot be undone.
runs.followUpDescription: Create a related run with resumed, fresh, or resent context.
runs.importedCollisionWarning: This externally managed Session is active in a worktree currently leased by another managed Session. It cannot be concurrency-controlled by this application.
runs.linkedItemsDescription: Source Plan, parent, and child runs related to this run.
runs.noRollbackChanges: No worktree content changes will be undone.
runs.pausedFollowUpDescription: Starting a resumed child cancels this paused Session and atomically transfers its worktree lease.
runs.planMutationWarning: This read-only Plan unexpectedly changed the selected worktree.
runs.promptPlaceholder: Describe what the agent should do…
runs.revisionDescription: The provider will be interrupted and the selected worktree restored to its checkpoint immediately before this question. Later activity remains in the superseded audit history.
runs.resendPrompt: Edit the original prompt before resending it without prior context.
runs.startDescription: Start a managed Plan or Session in the selected worktree.
runs.stashBeforeRollback: Stash current tracked and untracked changes before restoring
runs.steerDescription: Send guidance to the active provider turn. Guidance waits behind pending questions.
runs.steerPlaceholder: Add guidance for the running agent…
runs.unrestrictedWarning: Sessions run without approval prompts and with unrestricted access while holding an exclusive worktree lease.
runs.webSearchDescription: Enable the provider's native web-search tool when supported.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Review notification history and choose how each event reaches you.
notifications.deliverySetupDescription: Browser permission and Web Push subscription are configured separately for this browser.
notifications.browserUnsupported: Browser alerts are not supported here.
notifications.pushUnsupported: Web Push requires a supported browser and a secure HTTPS context.
notifications.subscriptionCount: {count, plural, one {# subscribed browser} other {# subscribed browsers}}
notifications.subscribedBrowsersDescription: Send a test notification or remove browsers that should no longer receive Web Push.
notifications.noSubscribedBrowsers: No browsers are subscribed to Web Push.
notifications.removeBrowserDescription: This browser will stop receiving Web Push notifications. It can subscribe again later.
notifications.permissionRequired: Notification permission is required for Web Push.
notifications.pushKeyUnavailable: The Web Push public key is unavailable.
notifications.pushSubscriptionInvalid: The browser returned an invalid push subscription.
notifications.iosInstallHelp: On iPhone or iPad, install this app from Share → Add to Home Screen before subscribing to Web Push.
notifications.preferencesDescription: Channels are independent. Enabling Browser and Web Push can produce both alerts.
notifications.deleteAllDescription: This permanently deletes every notification from the database and cannot be undone.
notifications.clearSidebarDescription: Notifications will be dismissed from the sidebar but remain in notification history.

permissions.denied: Permission is blocked in browser settings.

IOS_BUILD_FAILED.title: iOS build failed
IOS_BUILD_FAILED.description: An iOS build finishes with a failure.

GITHUB_ACTIONS_SUCCEEDED.title: GitHub Actions succeeded
GITHUB_ACTIONS_SUCCEEDED.description: A GitHub Actions workflow finishes successfully.

GITHUB_ACTIONS_FAILED.title: GitHub Actions failed
GITHUB_ACTIONS_FAILED.description: A GitHub Actions workflow finishes with a failure.

credentialEncryption.title: Database credentials are not encrypted
credentialEncryption.description: Secrets stored in the application database are currently plaintext. Generate a 32-byte key with this command:
credentialEncryption.instructionsSuffix: in the server environment, then restart the server. Existing plaintext credential rows are encrypted together after restart.
credentialEncryption.backup: Back up this key securely and retain it for the lifetime of the encrypted rows. Losing or changing it makes those credentials unreadable.

credentials.title: Credentials
credentials.description: Review the active credential backend, protection status, and a metadata-only inventory. Secret values are never shown here.
credentials.loadFailed: Credential inventory could not be loaded
credentials.storageDescription: Runtime configuration and safe location details for the selected backend.
credentials.inventoryDescription: This table contains identifiers and protection metadata only. Manage each value in its owning settings form.
credentials.emptyDescription: Credentials appear here after they are saved from an owning settings form.

warningTitles.KEYCHAIN_UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM: Keychain is unsupported on this host

warnings.DATABASE_UNENCRYPTED: Database credential payloads are stored as plaintext until an encryption key is configured.
warnings.CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY_INVALID: CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY must be strict base64 encoding of exactly 32 bytes. Fix it and restart the server; plaintext fallback is disabled when an invalid key is present.
warnings.CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY_MISSING: Encrypted credential rows exist, but the encryption key is missing. Restore the original key and restart the server.
warnings.CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY_MISMATCH: The configured key does not match existing encrypted rows. Restore the original key and restart the server.
warnings.CREDENTIAL_DATA_INVALID: One or more credential records contain invalid metadata and cannot be used.
warnings.CREDENTIAL_STORAGE_TYPE_INVALID: CREDENTIAL_STORAGE_TYPE must be database, vault, or keychain.
warnings.CREDENTIAL_STORE_UNAVAILABLE: The credential store could not be initialized. Review the server configuration and logs.
warnings.VAULT_CONFIGURATION_INVALID: Vault cannot be initialized with the current environment variables. Review the Vault address, headers, path, and TLS settings.
warnings.VAULT_INSECURE_HTTP: Vault traffic, secret payloads, and authentication headers are not protected in transit over HTTP.
warnings.VAULT_TLS_VERIFICATION_DISABLED: VAULT_SKIP_VERIFY allows untrusted certificates and exposes Vault traffic to interception.
warnings.KEYCHAIN_UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM: macOS Keychain is available only when the Next.js host runs on macOS. The server remains available, but credential operations will fail.
warnings.BACKEND_MISMATCH: {count} credential item(s) were recorded with another backend. Re-enter them through their owning settings forms; values are not moved or deleted automatically.

agents.title: Agents
agents.description: Manage enrolled Macs and durable jobs from one control plane.
agents.enrollmentDescription: Run this on the Mac after installing control-agent. The Mac connects outbound; it does not open a listening port.
agents.headersDescription: These values are added only to this command and saved in the agent’s owner-only local configuration.
agents.copyFailed: Could not copy the command. Select and copy it manually.
agents.emptyDescription: Create an enrollment command to pair your first Mac.

usage.title: Usage
usage.description: Review token usage and cost across every enrolled Mac.
usage.noAgentsDescription: Enroll a Mac before collecting usage.
usage.noCompatibleDescription: Update control-agent on an online Mac to add the ccusage capability.
usage.noOnlineDescription: Connect an enrolled Mac before collecting usage.
usage.collectionFailedDescription: No agent returned a valid ccusage report. Review the agent errors above and try again.
usage.zeroUsageDescription: The agents reported successfully, but ccusage found no daily usage.
usage.noUsageInRangeDescription: Choose a longer date range to see older usage.
usage.costChartDescription: Stacked USD usage for each day in the selected range.

agentDetail.generalInformationDescription: Hardware and resource usage update with each agent heartbeat.
agentDetail.capabilitiesDescription: Search the capabilities advertised by this agent, then expand one to invoke it and inspect its live response.
agentDetail.eventCapabilityDescription: This event requests an immediate codebase reconciliation and does not accept a payload.
agentDetail.invokeOffline: Connect this agent before invoking a capability.
agentDetail.noCapabilityResponse: Invoke the capability to see its response.
agentDetail.codebasesDescription: Registered codebases hosted by this agent.
agentDetail.noCodebases: No codebases are registered on this agent.
agentDetail.runDescription: Starts the allow-listed cloudflared handler on this Mac. It keeps running in the agent service when you leave this page.
agentDetail.noJobs: No jobs have run on this agent.
agentDetail.cadenceSettingsDescription: Tune how often this agent performs recurring control-plane and repository work. Scan and fetch start with the global codebase defaults; saving sets this agent’s values. All values are in seconds.
agentDetail.codebaseScanCadenceHelp: Inspects registered repositories and reports branch, sync, availability, and worktree state. Choose an integer from {min} to {max}.
agentDetail.jobReconciliationCadenceHelp: Queries the control plane for queued, running, or cancelled jobs so work recovers after missed events or an agent restart. Choose an integer from {min} to {max}.
agentDetail.gitFetchCadenceHelp: Runs git fetch origin when a registered codebase has not fetched within this interval. Choose an integer from {min} to {max}.
agentDetail.heartbeatCadenceHelp: Reports connection liveness, agent inventory, and current resource usage to the control plane. Choose an integer from {min} to {max}.
agentDetail.baseRepoDirectoryDescription: Choose the folder on this agent that contains its repositories. Worktree paths inside it are shown relative to this folder.
agentDetail.directoryBrowsingUnavailable: Connect this agent and update it if needed to browse directories.
agentDetail.derivedDataLocationDescription: Choose where this agent stores Xcode DerivedData.
agentDetail.derivedDataAbsoluteHelp: Enter an absolute path on this agent or choose an existing directory below.
agentDetail.derivedDataRelativeHelp: This folder is checked beneath every active tracked worktree.
agentDetail.buildsDirectoryDescription: Build logs, copied products, archives, and exports are stored in one folder per build. The default is the Base repository directory with /Builds appended unless overridden here.
agentDetail.deleteAgentDescription: Permanently delete {name} and all of its jobs and codebase registrations. This cannot be undone.

buildData.title: Build Data
buildData.description: Inspect and clean up Xcode DerivedData across enrolled agents.
buildData.derivedDataDescription: Per-project build products, indexes, logs, and shared Xcode caches.
buildData.deviceSupportDescription: Xcode symbols and support files retained for iOS versions connected to each agent.
buildData.selectAllDeviceSupport: Select all iOS Device Support entries
buildData.noDeviceSupport: No iOS Device Support folders were found.
buildData.deleteDeviceSupportDescription: Permanently delete {count} selected Device Support folders? Xcode can recreate them when a matching device is connected.
buildData.emptyDescription: No configured DerivedData directories currently contain entries.
buildData.deleteDescription: Permanently delete {count} selected Derived Data entries? This cannot be undone.
buildData.historyDescription: Derived Data removed from enrolled agents during the last 90 days.
buildData.clearHistoryDescription: Delete all retained Derived Data deletion records? This does not affect files on any agent.
buildData.noHistory: No Derived Data has been deleted yet.

cacheServerSettings.title: Actions Cache Server
cacheServerSettings.description: Connect to a GitHub Actions Cache Server to manage its cache entries.
cacheServerSettings.connectionSucceeded: Connected to the cache server successfully.
cacheServerSettings.baseUrlHelp: The management API base URL, including any path prefix.
cacheServerSettings.setupHelp: Get the management API base URL and API key from the cache server's deployment configuration or administrator. The configured key is sent as the x-api-key header.
cacheServerSettings.apiKeyPlaceholder: Enter the cache server API key
cacheServerSettings.apiKeyPlaceholderConfigured: Leave blank to keep the current key
cacheServerSettings.apiKeyHelp: Sent as the x-api-key header on every request.
cacheServerSettings.apiKeyKeepHelp: A key is stored. Enter a new value to replace it.
cacheServerSettings.headersHelp: Header values are stored locally, sent with every cache server request, and never returned to the browser.
cacheServerSettings.headersSetupHelp: Add custom headers only when an authentication proxy requires them; get their names and values from the proxy administrator.
cacheServerSettings.headerValuePlaceholderConfigured: Leave blank to keep the current value
cacheServerSettings.confirmRemoveDescription: The connection details and API key will be deleted. The Actions Cache page will be hidden until it is reconfigured.

cacheServer.title: Actions Cache
cacheServer.description: Browse and manage entries on the GitHub Actions Cache Server.
cacheServer.filtersDescription: Narrow cache entries by key, version, scope, or repository.
cacheServer.confirmDeleteMatchingDescription: Every cache entry matching the applied filters will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
cacheServer.entriesDescription: Cache entries stored on the server.
cacheServer.noEntries: No cache entries match the current filters.
cacheServer.notConfiguredDescription: Configure the GitHub Actions Cache Server on the Settings page to manage its entries.
cacheServer.detailDescription: The full cache entry and the storage location that backs it.
cacheServer.entryNotFound: This cache entry no longer exists.
cacheServer.confirmDeleteLocationDescription: The storage folder and its data will be removed. This action cannot be undone.
cacheServer.noLocation: No storage location is linked to this entry.
cacheServer.matchDescription: Find the entry the cache server would restore for a set of keys.
cacheServer.noMatch: No cache entry matched the provided keys.

jiraTickets.title: Jira Tickets
jiraTickets.description: Browse saved Jira projects, boards, and JQL queries by status.
jiraTickets.emptyProjectsDescription: Configure Jira in Settings, then add a project and its first source.
jiraTickets.emptySourcesDescription: Add a named JQL query or Jira board URL to load tickets.
jiraTickets.staleWarning: Jira could not be reached, so this view is showing expired cached data.
jiraTickets.truncatedWarning: This source has more than 1,000 tickets. Refine it to see the remaining results.
jiraTickets.manageDescription: Projects come from Jira. Each source is validated before it is saved.
jiraTickets.confirmRemoveProject: Remove this project and all of its saved sources?
jiraTickets.displaySettingsDescription: These settings apply only to this project.
jiraTickets.hideCompletedTicketsDescription: Choose which project statuses count as completed.
jiraTickets.branchNamingDescription: This function is used when a worktree branch is created from a ticket in this project.
jiraTickets.branchNamingFunctionHelp: Receives ticketKey, type, title, and alreadyTaken. Return a valid Git branch name; alreadyTaken is omitted on the first call.
jiraTickets.confirmDeleteSource: Delete this source and its related cache entries?
jiraTickets.staleTicket: Some ticket data is stale because Jira could not be reached.
jiraTickets.ticketWorktreeDescription: Create a new worktree on an agent, or create the ticket branch and switch an existing worktree.
jiraTickets.noWorktreeDestinations: No online, compatible destinations are available.
jiraTickets.chooseWorktreeDestination: Choose where the ticket branch should be created.
jiraTickets.ticketBranchChanged: The ticket branch was created and the worktree switched to it.
jiraTickets.ticketStashRetryHelp: Git could not switch with the current changes. Stash tracked and untracked changes, then retry.

jiraTicketDetail.notFoundDescription: The Jira ticket could not be loaded.
jiraTicketDetail.descriptionHistoryDescription: Select two versions from Jira activity to compare their text.
jiraTicketDetail.noDescriptionHistory: No previous description versions are available.
jiraTicketDetail.selectVersions: Select two different versions to compare.
jiraTicketDetail.editDetailsDescription: Only fields Jira allows you to edit are shown.

jiraCache.title: Jira Cache
jiraCache.description: Inspect cached Jira data, API usage, latency, and errors.
jiraCache.confirmClear: Clear every Jira response and cached ticket? API call history will be kept.
jiraCache.operationsDescription: Counts by operation and result source for each rolling window.
jiraCache.noMetrics: No Jira API activity in the last 24 hours.
jiraCache.recentDescription: Every cached Jira SDK operation, newest first.
jiraCache.noCalls: No Jira API calls have been recorded.
jiraCache.ticketsDescription: Materialized summary, detail, and comment coverage for Jira issues.
jiraCache.confirmDeleteTicket: Delete {issueKey} and every cache entry that references it?

jiraCacheDetail.confirmDelete: Delete {issueKey} and its related cache entries?
jiraCacheDetail.notFound: This ticket is not in the Jira cache.
jiraCacheDetail.notFetched: This data level has not been fetched.
jiraCacheDetail.noEntries: No cache entries reference this ticket.

jiraSettings.title: Settings
jiraSettings.description: Configure the Jira Cloud account used by this application.
jiraSettings.siteUrlHelp: Use the base *.atlassian.net URL for your Jira site.
jiraSettings.accountHelp: Open Jira in your browser and copy only its https://*.atlassian.net origin. Use the email address for the same Atlassian account.
jiraSettings.tokenPlaceholderConfigured: Leave blank to keep the saved token
jiraSettings.tokenHelp: The token is stored in the local SQLite database and is never returned to the browser.
jiraSettings.tokenKeepHelp: Leave this blank unless you want to replace the saved token.
jiraSettings.tokenAcquireHelp: Create a standard Atlassian API token that can authenticate directly to this Jira site.
jiraSettings.removed: Jira credentials removed and cached Jira data cleared.
jiraSettings.confirmSiteChange: Changing the Jira site removes all saved Jira projects, sources, and cached data. Continue?
jiraSettings.confirmRemove: Remove the saved email and API token and clear cached Jira data?

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configure development tools, Apple services, and external integrations.
settings.developmentGroupDescription: Choose the tools used with local checkouts and worktrees.
settings.appleGroupDescription: Manage iOS enrollment, App Store Connect, and APNs credentials.
settings.integrationsGroupDescription: Connect the Jira and GitHub accounts used throughout the application.

editorSettings.title: Worktree editor
editorSettings.description: Choose which VS Code edition opens worktrees, or hide editor actions.
editorSettings.label: Editor
editorSettings.setupHelp: Install the selected edition on the Mac that hosts the worktree:

githubSettings.title: GitHub credentials
githubSettings.description: Connect the account used to load pull requests and GitHub Actions.
githubSettings.tokenPlaceholder: Paste a GitHub personal access token
githubSettings.tokenPlaceholderConfigured: Leave blank to keep the saved token
githubSettings.tokenHelp: Grant pull-request read and write access plus Actions read access in the repositories you want to use. The token is used for pull request views, review-thread actions, and workflow-run details.
githubSettings.tokenKeepHelp: Leave this blank unless you want to replace the saved token.
githubSettings.tokenAcquireHelp: Create a fine-grained token limited to the repositories used here. Grant Actions: read, Checks: read, Pull requests: read and write, and Contents: read and write for merging. Email addresses: read is optional for commit-email choices.
githubSettings.pollInterval: Actions notification and Auto Retry interval (seconds)
githubSettings.pollIntervalHelp: Controls Auto Retry polling and, when a signed GitHub App webhook is unavailable, notification polling. Choose an integer from 30 to 3600.
githubSettings.removed: GitHub credentials removed. Managed repositories were kept.
githubSettings.confirmRemoveDescription: The saved token will be removed. Managed repository and Jira regex settings will be kept.

githubAppSettings.title: GitHub App pipeline retries
githubAppSettings.description: Use your own GitHub App to rerun GitHub Actions workflows.
githubAppSettings.stepRegister: Register a uniquely named GitHub App, such as “AI Environment Check Rerunner,” from
githubAppSettings.stepHomepageSuffix: as the Homepage URL. Leave the callback URL empty.
githubAppSettings.stepPermissions: Set Repository permissions → Actions to Read and write. Metadata remains read-only automatically; Checks permission is not required.
githubAppSettings.stepWebhook: Enable webhooks, select the Workflow run event, and use
githubAppSettings.stepWebhookSuffix: as the Webhook URL. Saving below generates the secret and configures this URL through GitHub.
githubAppSettings.stepInstall: Create the app, generate a private key, then install it on only the repositories this deployment should control.
githubAppSettings.stepInstallationId: After installation, open the app’s Configure page. The installation ID is the final number in its URL, for example
githubAppSettings.stepInstallationIdSuffix: . Replace Organization-name with your organization; copy the final ID value.
githubAppSettings.stepCredentials: Copy the App ID and installation ID, then paste them and the downloaded PEM below.
githubAppSettings.webhookUrlHelp: Override the public HTTPS endpoint when this deployment is behind a proxy or tunnel.
githubAppSettings.pemDropHint: Drop a .pem file here, or paste the key below.
githubAppSettings.pemFileLoaded: Loaded {filename}. Save and verify to use this key.
githubAppSettings.pemFileInvalid: Drop a valid .pem file containing an RSA private key.
githubAppSettings.pemFileReadError: The dropped PEM file could not be read.
githubAppSettings.privateKeyPlaceholder: Paste the downloaded GitHub App PEM
githubAppSettings.privateKeyPlaceholderConfigured: Leave blank to keep the configured private key
githubAppSettings.privateKeyHelp: The key is sent only to the Next.js server and is never returned to the browser.
githubAppSettings.privateKeyKeepHelp: A private key is configured. Paste a replacement PEM only when rotating it.
githubAppSettings.webhookNeedsSetup: GitHub App credentials are verified, but GitHub reports that webhooks are not enabled. Enable webhooks, select the Workflow run event, then save again.
githubAppSettings.webhookUnavailable: Webhook unavailable until this deployment has a public HTTPS origin; personal-token polling remains available.
githubAppSettings.rotated: GitHub App private key rotated and verified.
githubAppSettings.removed: GitHub App credentials removed. The personal token and managed repositories were kept.
githubAppSettings.confirmRemoveDescription: The App ID, installation ID, and private key will be removed. Pull request credentials and managed repositories will be kept.

polling.title: Polling
polling.description: Inspect background server and agent operations, their cadence, freshness, and failures.

pullRequests.title: Pull Requests
pullRequests.description: Review your GitHub pull requests and monitored repositories in one place.
pullRequests.credentialsRequired: Connect GitHub to view pull requests
pullRequests.credentialsRequiredDescription: Add a personal access token in Settings before loading GitHub data.
pullRequests.truncated: GitHub search returns at most 1,000 results. Refine your workload on GitHub to see anything beyond this limit.
pullRequests.emptyDescription: No pull requests currently match this view.
pullRequests.mergePullRequestDescription: Merge {repository} #{number} after reviewing the commit details.

retryUnavailable.GITHUB_APP_NOT_CONFIGURED: Configure and verify a GitHub App in Settings to retry this workflow.
retryUnavailable.NOT_COMPLETED: Only completed workflows can be retried.
retryUnavailable.NOT_GITHUB_ACTIONS: Only GitHub Actions workflow runs can be retried.
retryUnavailable.WORKFLOW_RUN_UNAVAILABLE: GitHub did not provide a workflow run for this check suite.

pullRequests.manageDescription: Choose repository tabs and configure how Jira keys are parsed from pull request titles.
pullRequests.manageCredentialsRequired: Configure GitHub credentials before adding repositories.
pullRequests.jiraKeyRegexHelp: The first capture group is used when present; otherwise the entire match is used. Clear this field to disable Jira key parsing.
pullRequests.defaultJiraKeyRegexHelp: Used for every managed repository without its own override. The first capture group is used when present.
pullRequests.jiraKeyRegexOverrideHelp: Leave blank to inherit the default ticket key regex.
pullRequests.invalidRegex: Enter a valid Jira key regular expression.
pullRequests.confirmRemoveRepositoryDescription: Its pull request tab and Jira regex configuration will be removed.

githubComments.title: Comments
githubComments.description: Review and respond to GitHub review threads on your open pull requests.
githubComments.credentialsDescription: Add a personal access token with pull-request read and write access in Settings.
githubComments.noPullRequestMatches: No open pull requests match your search.
githubComments.truncated: GitHub search returns at most 1,000 pull requests. Some review threads may not be shown.
githubComments.emptyDescription: No review threads match the selected pull request and filters.
githubComments.reviewThreadsDescription: {count, plural, =0 {No review threads match the current filters.} one {# review thread matches the current filters.} other {# review threads match the current filters.}}
githubComments.author: Author
githubComments.replyUnavailable: GitHub does not allow this account to reply to this thread.

pullRequestDetail.notFoundDescription: This pull request is unavailable or the configured GitHub token cannot access it.
pullRequestDetail.noCommentsDescription: This pull request does not have any review threads.
pullRequestDetail.noJobs: No jobs were reported for this run.
pullRequestDetail.noSteps: No steps have been reported for this job yet.
pullRequestDetail.author: Author

actionsPage.title: GitHub Actions
actionsPage.description: Monitor workflow runs across registered GitHub codebases.
actionsPage.credentialsRequiredDescription: Add a personal access token with Actions read access in Settings.
actionsPage.noCodebasesDescription: Register a codebase with a github.com origin to view its workflow runs.
actionsPage.emptyDescription: GitHub did not return any workflow runs for this repository selection.
actionsPage.forceCancelDescription: Force cancellation bypasses conditions that normally prevent GitHub from stopping this run. Use it only when normal cancellation is stuck.
actionsPage.noJobs: No jobs were reported for this run.
actionsPage.noSteps: No steps have been reported for this job yet.

githubAutomation.title: Configure Auto Retry
githubAutomation.description: Automatically rerun selected GitHub Actions workflows or jobs.
githubAutomation.credentialsRequired: Configure a personal access token and verify a GitHub App with Actions write access before creating Auto Retry rules.
githubAutomation.allWorkflows: All workflows, including workflows added later
githubAutomation.noWorkflows: No GitHub Actions workflows were found.
githubAutomation.limitError: Enter a retry limit from 1 to 100.
githubAutomation.targetRequired: Select at least one workflow or job.
githubAutomation.noJobs: No jobs were reported for this attempt.

codebases.title: Codebases
codebases.description: Track Git repositories and checkouts across every enrolled agent.
codebases.emptyDescription: Add an existing Git repository from an online agent.
codebases.skipped: Skipped {count} offline, unsupported, or unavailable codebases.
codebases.addDescription: Browse an agent, inspect a Git repository, and confirm its origin before saving.
codebases.truncated: Only the first 1,000 folders are shown.
codebases.existingOrigin: This origin already uses the shared name “{name}”.
codebases.name: Repository name
codebases.repositoryNotFoundDescription: This logical repository may no longer have a registered checkout.
codebases.repositorySharedDescription: These details, build configurations, and allowed scripts belong to the logical repository and apply across every agent checkout and worktree.
codebases.repositoryDetailsDescription: Manage settings shared by every registered checkout of this repository.
codebases.autoRetryDescription: Configure durable retry rules for GitHub Actions workflows in this repository.
codebases.autoRetryGitHubOnly: Auto Retry is available only for repositories hosted on GitHub.
codebases.skillGroupsHelp: Choose the project skill groups synchronized into every checkout and worktree for this repository.
codebases.keepBaseBranchUpToDate: Keep base branch up to date
codebases.keepBaseBranchUpToDateHelp: After a successful fetch, fast-forward the base branch when there are no staged changes. This repository setting applies to every agent.
codebases.confirmRemoveDescription: The registration for {folder} will be removed. The folder and all of its files will remain untouched on the agent.
codebases.settingsDescription: Set how often updated agents inspect registered folders and report Git status.
codebases.refreshIntervalHelp: Choose an integer from {min} to {max}. Each agent waits this long after a scan completes. Git remotes are not contacted.
codebases.fetchIntervalHelp: Choose an integer from {min} to {max}. Each agent fetches origin when this interval is due.
codebases.jiraBranchRegexHelp: The first capture group is used when present; otherwise the entire branch match is used.

codebaseDetail.notFoundDescription: This codebase registration may have been removed.
codebaseDetail.offlineReadOnly: This agent is offline. Stored branch information is available read-only until it reconnects.
codebaseDetail.unsupportedReadOnly: Update this agent to inspect and manage branches and stashes. Stored branch information is shown read-only.
codebaseDetail.branchesTruncated: Only the first 1,000 branches are shown.
codebaseDetail.noRemoteBranches: No origin branches found. Fetch origin and try again.
codebaseDetail.confirmDirtySwitchDescription: Staged, unstaged, and untracked changes will be saved in a new stash before switching to {branch}. The stash will be preserved.
codebaseDetail.confirmDirtyCheckoutTitle: Stash changes and check out this branch?
codebaseDetail.confirmDirtyCheckoutDescription: Staged, unstaged, and untracked changes will be saved in a new stash before checking out {branch}. The stash will be preserved.
codebaseDetail.confirmDeleteRemoteBranchDescription: origin/{branch} will be permanently deleted. Any same-named local branch and worktrees will remain untouched.
codebaseDetail.confirmDeleteBranchDescription: Git will safely delete {branch} only if its commits are already merged. This cannot delete the current, default, or another worktree's branch.
codebaseDetail.noStashesDescription: This checkout does not currently have any Git stashes.
codebaseDetail.emptyPatch: This stash has no text patch to display.
codebaseDetail.patchTruncated: This patch was truncated at 256 KiB.
codebaseDetail.stashesTruncated: Only the first 200 stashes are shown.
codebaseDetail.confirmDeleteStashDescription: {stash} will be permanently removed. Apply it first if you need to preserve its changes.

worktrees.title: Worktrees
worktrees.description: Manage primary and linked Git worktrees across every enrolled agent.
worktrees.agentUnsupported: Update the agent to enable iOS builds.
worktrees.iosBuildNotConfigured: Add an iOS App project and build configuration in repository settings first.
worktrees.searchWorktreesPlaceholder: Search branches, agents, repositories, tickets, or codebases…
worktrees.noMatchingWorktreesDescription: Try a different search or filter.
worktrees.selectCodebaseHelp: Choose the agent checkout that will own this worktree.
worktrees.noEligibleCodebases: No online, compatible repository checkouts are available.
worktrees.moveDescription: Push {branch} and check it out on another agent.
worktrees.noMoveDestinations: No compatible destination checkouts are available.
worktrees.dirtyDestinationWarning: This destination has uncommitted changes. Git will carry them onto the incoming branch when possible.
worktrees.moveCompletedWithWarning: The destination is ready, but source cleanup needs attention.
worktrees.moveCancelled: Move cancelled. The source branch remains pushed and the source worktree was kept.
worktrees.moveNeedsStash: Destination changes prevent Git from switching branches.
worktrees.moveStashHelp: Stash tracked and untracked destination changes, continue the move, and leave the stash for manual restoration.

moveStatus.SUCCEEDED_WITH_WARNING: Move complete with a cleanup warning.

moveBlocked.agent: The source agent is offline or must be updated.
moveBlocked.busy: Another operation is active for this repository.
moveBlocked.detached: A named source branch is required.
moveBlocked.dirty: Commit or discard source changes before moving.
moveBlocked.behind: Pull the origin changes before moving.
moveBlocked.diverged: Resolve the diverged origin branch before moving.
moveBlocked.unknown: The branch cannot currently be verified as safely pushable.
moveBlocked.destination: No compatible destination agent is currently available.

worktrees.deleteWorktreeDescription: Permanently remove {path} and its local branch.
worktrees.deleteWorktreeWarning: All staged, unstaged, and untracked changes in this worktree will be permanently lost.
worktrees.defaultRemoteProtected: The repository default branch cannot be deleted from origin.
worktrees.stashRetryHelp: Git could not switch with the current changes. Stash tracked and untracked changes, retry, and leave the stash for manual restoration.
worktrees.emptyDescription: Add a codebase and connect its agent to discover its primary and linked worktrees.
worktrees.manageTagsDescription: Create reusable global tags and choose their badge colors.
worktrees.noCommits: No commits differ from the base branch.
worktrees.truncated: Only the first supported results are shown.
worktrees.hiddenDescription: Missing worktrees are retained for seven days. Rediscovery restores their saved metadata.

worktreeDetail.notFoundDescription: This worktree is missing, hidden, or no longer available.
worktreeDetail.inspectionOffline: The agent is offline. Saved worktree metadata remains available, but commits and changes cannot be inspected.
worktreeDetail.inspectionUnavailable: This worktree is unavailable for live inspection.
worktreeDetail.inspectionBusy: Commits and changes will refresh after the active worktree operation finishes.
worktreeDetail.inspectionUnsupported: Update the agent to inspect commits and changes.
worktreeDetail.inspectionFailed: Commits and changes could not be loaded.
worktreeDetail.head: HEAD commit
worktreeDetail.diffTruncated: Some changed files are not shown because the result was truncated.
worktreeDetail.noCoverageReports: No worktree coverage reports have been generated.
worktreeDetail.diffTooLarge: Diff exceeds the 2 MiB display limit

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Browse, configure, and run built-in and external MCP tools.
tools.searchPlaceholder: Search tool names, descriptions, or servers…
tools.serversDescription: Manage Streamable HTTP and legacy SSE tool providers.
tools.noServers: No external MCP servers are configured.
tools.name: Name
tools.confirmDeleteDescription: Delete {name} and its saved custom headers?
tools.catalogDescription: Expand a tool, enter its parameters, and inspect the complete response.
tools.serverDialogDescription: Connection details are used only by the server-side MCP client.
tools.prefixHelp: The prefix is applied literally when tools are displayed and invoked.
tools.headersHelp: Header values are saved locally and never returned to the browser.
tools.keepHeaderValue: Leave blank to keep the saved value
tools.noResponse: Run the tool to see its response.
tools.jsonObjectPlaceholder: Enter key/value pairs as a JSON object

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Manage reusable AI skill packages across the database, clients, agents, and repositories.
skills.searchPlaceholder: Search names, descriptions, groups, repositories, agents, or paths…
skills.databaseSkillsDescription: Canonical skill packages stored in the control plane.
skills.clientSkillsDescription: Skill packages discovered in the selected AI client.
skills.name: Name
skills.noSkills: No database skills match this search.
skills.noClientSkills: No skills were discovered for this client.
skills.settingsDescription: Choose which configured clients participate and whether project groups reconcile automatically.
skills.autoSyncHelp: Keep assigned groups current in every registered checkout and worktree.
skills.editorDescription: Edit metadata and every file in this skill package.
skills.deleteSkillDescription: Delete {name} from the database and remove its managed copies during sync?
skills.syncGloballyHelp: Include this skill in personal/global client synchronization.
skills.binaryFile: Binary file. Download or replace it to edit its contents.
skills.duplicateFile: A file with that path already exists.
skills.groupsDescription: Bundle project-only skills and assign them to repositories.
skills.groupsTableDescription: Project skill groups and their assigned skills and repositories.
skills.newGroupDescription: Create the group, then choose its skills and repositories.
skills.groupSettingsDescription: Manage membership, repository assignments, and deployment.
skills.deleteGroupDescription: Delete {name} and remove copies no longer required by another group?
skills.syncGroupName: Synchronizing the {name} group across assigned repositories.
skills.syncAllDescription: Reconciling database, personal, and project skills across all agents.
skills.syncAgentsDescription: Connection and synchronization status for every participating agent.
skills.syncChangesDescription: Review the changes and resolve conflicts before applying this sync.
skills.manualResolutionDescription: Compare complete packages and edit the canonical result, including text, binary, and executable files.
skills.binaryPreview: Binary file; compare size and executable metadata.
skills.missingFile: This version does not contain the selected file.
skills.missingResultFile: Add this file to the result or select another package file.

builds.title: Builds
builds.description: Configure, run, inspect, deploy, and export iOS builds across codebases and worktrees.
builds.deleteBuildsDescription: This permanently deletes the selected build records and their build folders.
builds.emptyDescription: Start a build from a worktree to create the first immutable build record.
builds.noScriptsDescription: Create reusable JavaScript hooks, then allow them for individual repositories.
builds.scriptDescription: Trusted Node.js ESM hooks run from the selected worktree root with a minimal environment.
builds.name: Name
builds.preBuildScriptHelp: Default-export a function that receives buildId, branch, destination, action, and the telemetry URL snapshot.
builds.postBuildScriptHelp: Default-export a function that also receives buildFolder, failed, cancelled, errorCode, error, and the same telemetry URL snapshot.
builds.startBuildDescription: Choose one saved configuration and destination. The command runs from this worktree root.
builds.configurationInvalid: The saved source, scheme, or configuration is unavailable in this worktree.
builds.noConfigurations: Create a valid iOS build configuration in repository settings before starting a build.
builds.selectPriorBuildForTesting: Select a compatible Build for Testing
builds.noPriorBuildForTesting: Run a successful Build for Testing with this configuration first.
builds.parseTestResults: Parse test results after the build
builds.advancedSettingsNotCaptured: Advanced settings were not captured for this build.
builds.startWorktreeCoverageDescription: Run tests with coverage and measure executable lines changed in this branch and working tree.
builds.startBuildFailed: The build could not be started.
builds.testResultsFailed: Test results could not be parsed.
builds.coverageReportNotFoundDescription: Generate a coverage report from the build details page first.
builds.overridesInvalid: Build-setting overrides must be a JSON object.
builds.advancedJsonInvalid: Advanced settings must be valid JSON.
builds.buildNotFoundDescription: This build record does not exist or is no longer available.
builds.deleteBuildDescription: This permanently deletes the build record and its build folder.
builds.installRequiresHttps: Over-the-air installation requires a public HTTPS address. Open this page over HTTPS or set PUBLIC_BASE_URL.
builds.installNotSupportedForAppStore: App Store Connect builds cannot be installed over the air. Export with Debugging, Release Testing, or Enterprise instead.
builds.installMissingBundleIdentifier: The bundle identifier is unavailable. Export the archive again to enable installation.
builds.installOpenOnDevice: Open this page on an iPhone or iPad to install.
builds.configureBuilds: Go to repository settings to configure builds
builds.noCompatibleDevices: No compatible connected destinations are available.
builds.noLogs: No log events have been reported.
builds.copyFailed: Could not copy to the clipboard.
builds.head: Commit
builds.noRunsOrExports: This build has not been run or exported.
builds.exportArchiveDescription: Export the captured .xcarchive locally without uploading it.
builds.noTeams: No development teams were found on the build agent.
builds.parseProvisioningProfilesHelp: Read the selected scheme’s signable targets and match installed profiles.
builds.parseProvisioningProfilesEmpty: Parse the project to replace JSON with one profile selector per app and extension.
builds.noSigningRequirements: No signable app or extension targets were found.
builds.selectSigningSourceFirst: Select an Xcode source, scheme, and configuration first.
builds.manualBundleIdentifierHelp: Add any bundle identifier that parsing missed, then choose from every available profile.
builds.noMatchingProfiles: No installed profile matches this bundle for the selected method.
builds.noArchiveBundles: No signable bundles were found in the archive.
builds.signingOptionsFailed: Could not read signing options from the agent.
builds.manageBuildNumber: Manage app version and build number
builds.localExportOnly: Exports are written locally as an IPA. These settings never upload to App Store Connect.
builds.signingAssetCoverageWarning: This identity is installed on {installed} of {total} signing-capable agents. Builds assigned elsewhere may fail to export.
builds.parseCheckoutDescription: Saved configurations are repository-wide. Source discovery, validation, and parse status use the selected agent checkout.
builds.addIosProjectDescription: Enable reusable Xcode build configurations for every checkout and worktree of this repository.
builds.sharedProjectDescription: Project definitions and allowed scripts are shared by the repository. Parse status below comes from this agent checkout.
builds.createScriptsOnBuildsPage: Create reusable scripts on the Builds page, then return here to allow them.
builds.configurationDescription: Select a repository-relative Xcode source and save its scheme, configuration, action, and optional advanced settings.
builds.selectSource: Select a project, workspace, or root Package.swift

devices.title: Devices
devices.description: Enroll iPhones and iPads, review connection history, and register approved devices with Apple.
devices.emptyDescription: Create an enrollment profile on an iPhone or iPad to collect its identifier.
devices.enrollDescription: Install a temporary signed profile to send this device’s development identifier securely.
devices.httpsRequired: Enrollment is disabled because this address is not public HTTPS. Use a publicly trusted HTTPS reverse proxy or configure PUBLIC_BASE_URL.
devices.collectUdid: The device UDID used for Apple development registration.
devices.collectProduct: The Apple product model reported by iOS.
devices.collectVersion: The installed iOS or iPadOS version.
devices.collectIp: The IP address observed during profile download and response, including which proxy header supplied it.
devices.deviceLabelHelp: Choose a recognizable label. Apple’s profile service cannot read the name assigned in Settings.
devices.consent: I consent to collection and storage of the device identifier, product, software version, and observed IP addresses for device management.
devices.rebuildWarning: This device is registered with Apple. Existing IPAs do not gain access automatically; create a new provisioning profile and export a new IPA.
devices.configureAppleFirst: Configure and verify App Store Connect credentials before registering this device.
devices.noIpHistory: No IP observations are stored for this device.
devices.noEnrollmentHistory: No enrollment attempts are stored for this device.
devices.firmwareDescription: Firmware versions and current signing status provided by IPSW.me.
devices.firmwareLoadFailed: Firmware information could not be loaded: {error}
devices.noFirmware: No firmware information is available for this device.
devices.registerDescription: The device will be reconciled by UDID and then added to your Apple Developer account if needed. This does not rebuild an IPA.
devices.rejectDescription: The device remains in local history and cannot be registered. Delete it locally and enroll it again if you want to reconsider it.
devices.deleteDescription: This deletes the local device, enrollment attempts, and IP history. Apple’s annual device registration is not removed.

iosDeviceSettings.title: iOS device enrollment
iosDeviceSettings.description: Configure signed enrollment profiles and optional Apple device registration.
iosDeviceSettings.profileDescription: These values identify the temporary profile shown in iOS Settings.
iosDeviceSettings.profileOrganizationHelp: Use a recognizable name; iOS displays this organization while the enrollment profile is installed.
iosDeviceSettings.profileIdentifierHelp: Use a stable reverse-DNS identifier, such as com.example.ai-development-environment.enrollment.
iosDeviceSettings.unverifiedWarning: The generated certificate is self-signed, so iOS shows the profile as Unverified. Review the displayed organization before installing it.
iosDeviceSettings.regenerateDescription: New enrollment profiles will use a new private key and certificate. Profiles already downloaded can still return their device response until their token expires.
iosDeviceSettings.signerRegenerated: A new profile signer was generated.
iosDeviceSettings.appleDescription: Use an API key with Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles access. The private key is stored server-side and never returned to the browser.
iosDeviceSettings.appleSetupHelp: In Users and Access → Integrations, create a Team API key with Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles access. Copy its Issuer ID and Key ID, then download and retain the .p8 file; Apple offers it only once.
iosDeviceSettings.openApiKeys: Open App Store Connect API keys
iosDeviceSettings.p8DropZone: App Store Connect private key drop zone
iosDeviceSettings.p8DropHint: Paste the key below, drop a .p8 file here, or choose a file.
iosDeviceSettings.privateKeyConfiguredPlaceholder: A private key is stored. Leave blank to keep it.
iosDeviceSettings.privateKeyHelp: Only ES256 PKCS#8 keys downloaded from App Store Connect are accepted.
iosDeviceSettings.privateKeyKeepHelp: Leave this blank to keep the stored key, or provide a new .p8 key to replace it.
iosDeviceSettings.clearDescription: Device registration will be disabled until another API key is saved and verified.
iosDeviceSettings.appleSaved: App Store Connect credentials saved and verified.
iosDeviceSettings.appleTestSucceeded: App Store Connect credentials verified successfully.
iosDeviceSettings.p8Invalid: Choose a valid .p8 file containing a PKCS#8 private key.
iosDeviceSettings.p8ReadError: The selected .p8 file could not be read.
iosDeviceSettings.p8Loaded: Loaded {filename}. Save and verify to use this key.

telemetry.consoleDescription: Inspect searchable console output collected from builds and devices.
telemetry.analyticsDescription: Inspect product analytics events and their parameters as they arrive.
telemetry.unifiedDescription: Review console logs and analytics events in one live timeline.
telemetry.advancedFiltersDescription: Combine reusable conditions across standard fields and parameter paths.
telemetry.manageColumnsDescription: Choose, order, reset, and save the columns used by this view.
telemetry.apiHelpDescription: Review the REST ingestion contract and copy a ready-to-use Markdown reference.
telemetry.settingsDescription: Configure auto-detected and overridden ingestion origins shared by every view.
telemetry.addSeparatorDescription: Insert a shared timeline boundary. The name is optional.
telemetry.exportAllDescription: Export every row matching the current search and filters.

provisioningProfiles.title: Provisioning Profiles
provisioningProfiles.description: Inspect, sync, import, and remove Apple signing assets across build agents and the Apple Developer portal.
provisioningProfiles.noRefreshAgents: No signing-capable agents are available to refresh.
provisioningProfiles.agentsDescription: Older agents remain visible but cannot receive signing operations until they are updated.
provisioningProfiles.uploadProfileDescription: Install one .mobileprovision on every selected agent.
provisioningProfiles.importIdentityDescription: Send an encrypted .p12 through one-time target-bound claims and import it for codesign.
provisioningProfiles.profilesDescription: Profiles are grouped across agents by UUID and content hash.
provisioningProfiles.deleteExpiredDescription: Remove all expired local provisioning profiles from the selected agents?
provisioningProfiles.name: Name
provisioningProfiles.deleteProfileDescription: Remove this profile from every listed agent. Xcode-managed profiles may be downloaded again.
provisioningProfiles.deleteIdentityDescription: Delete this identity and its private key from each listed agent login keychain.
provisioningProfiles.createPortalProfileDescription: Creates a profile from an existing bundle ID, certificate, and all applicable registered devices. Private keys are never generated.
provisioningProfiles.portalDeleteDescription: This immediately changes the Apple Developer account and can break signing on other machines.
provisioningProfiles.certificatesDescription: Code-signing identities grouped by certificate fingerprint across agents.
provisioningProfiles.profileNotFound: This provisioning profile is no longer installed on an agent.
provisioningProfiles.deviceListPendingRefresh: Refresh signing inventory to load the device identifiers stored in this profile.
provisioningProfiles.noProvisionedDevices: This distribution profile is not restricted to individual devices.

apnsDevices.title: APNs Devices
apnsDevices.description: App and MDM push-token registrations available for notification targeting.
apnsDevices.empty: No apps have registered an APNs token yet.
apnsDevices.renameDescription: Choose a label that makes this registration easy to target.
apnsDevices.deleteDescription: Delete this token registration and detach it from retained delivery history?
apnsDevices.apiHelpDescription: POST strict JSON to the public registration endpoint. Tokens may be HEX or Base64; MDM registrations also require pushMagic.

pushSettings.title: Push Notifications
pushSettings.tokenDescription: Recommended for apps, Live Activities, VoIP, and other non-MDM push types.
pushSettings.tokenSetupHelp: Create a key with Apple Push Notifications service (APNs) enabled, copy its Key ID, and download and retain the .p8 file; Apple offers it only once.
pushSettings.teamIdHelp: Copy the Team ID from your Apple Developer membership details.
pushSettings.chooseP8: Drop or choose the APNs .p8 key
pushSettings.replaceP8: Drop a new .p8 to rotate the key
pushSettings.removeTokenDescription: Pushes using token authentication will stop until a replacement .p8 is configured.
pushSettings.certificateDescription: Multiple topic-bound certificates are supported and are required for MDM wake-ups.
pushSettings.certificateSetupHelp: Create the appropriate push certificate, install it with its private key in Keychain Access, then export both as a password-protected .p12. Use the certificate topic (the app bundle ID or MDM topic) and select its sandbox or production environment.
pushSettings.name: Credential name
pushSettings.removeCertificateDescription: Sends using this certificate will fail after it is removed.

pushNotifications.title: Push Notifications
pushNotifications.description: Compose, validate, target, queue, and inspect Apple push notifications.
pushNotifications.editorDescription: APNs payload shape, headers, byte limits, and eligible devices update with the selected push type.
pushNotifications.subtitle: Subtitle
pushNotifications.mdmHelp: MDM uses the selected certificate and each registration’s PushMagic. Its payload contains mdm only and no aps dictionary.
pushNotifications.specializedHelp: Use Custom root JSON for the framework-specific {type} application payload; APNs headers and limits are still validated.
pushNotifications.broadcastDescription: Channel storage policy is immutable after creation.
pushNotifications.deleteChannelDescription: Delete this channel. Existing presets and history keep only its identifier.
pushNotifications.deleteHistoryDescription: Delete this batch, its payload snapshot, and recipient outcomes.
pushNotifications.clearHistoryDescription: Delete every completed send and draft? Active batches are preserved.
pushNotifications.name: Name
pushNotifications.deletePresetDescription: Delete this reusable payload and header preset.
pushNotifications.historyDescription: Sent batches, drafts, and per-recipient APNs outcomes.
pushNotifications.presetsDescription: Reusable notification content and headers; recipients are selected when sending.
pushNotifications.noPresets: No push notification presets saved yet.
